name: Death swallowed up in victory
notes: Paul, proclaiming the resurrection of the body, gathers two prophets into one triumph-song. Isaiah's promise that the LORD "will swallow up death in victory" is brought to pass when the corruptible puts on incorruption (Isa 25:8 -> 1Cor 15:54). To this Paul joins Hosea's address to the grave, keeping the exact double vocative "O death ... O grave" (Hos 13:14 -> 1Cor 15:55). Hosea voices it as the LORD's sentence of judgment upon death itself ("I will be thy plagues ... thy destruction"); Paul renders the same vantage as a victor's taunt over a defeated foe ("where is thy sting ... thy victory"). The shared address with the complementary wording is a matter for further study. The shared core is the certain abolition of death in the risen Christ.

links.label: swallow up death in victory ... Death is swallowed up in victory
